canonical_value: Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the image. The transient reactance of the generator is 0.1 pu, and the mechanical power input to it is constant at 1.0 pu. Due to some previous disturbance, the rotor angle (δ) is undergoing an undamped oscillation, with the maximum value of δ(t) equal to 130°. One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when δ(t) = 130° as shown in the image. What is the maximum value of the per unit line reactance such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping?
surface_forms: Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the image. The transient reactance of the generator is 0.1 pu, and the mechanical power input to it is constant at 1.0 pu. Due to some previous disturbance, the rotor angle (δ) is undergoing an undamped oscillation, with the maximum value of δ(t) equal to 130°. One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when δ(t) = 130° as shown in the image. What is the maximum value of the per unit line reactance such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping?

normalized_value.title: EEE-Bench 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text: Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the image. The transient reactance of the generator is 0.1 pu, and the mechanical power input to it is constant at 1.0 pu. Due to some previous disturbance, the rotor angle (δ) is undergoing an undamped oscillation, with the maximum value of δ(t) equal to 130°. One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when δ(t) = 130° as shown in the image. What is the maximum value of the per unit line reactance such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping?
